question: Does Scout's builder directory currently contain an exact builder record for “Strupey”?

golden.answer: No exact `Strupey` builder record was returned by Scout's builder directory when checked on 2026-07-13. That answers this closed-world directory-membership question at Scout's scope; it does not establish that the name has no history or footprint elsewhere. A broader ecosystem search is unnecessary unless the user expands the question beyond this directory.
keyFacts: Directly answers the exact Scout builder-directory membership question from the directory result.
Scopes the negative to Scout's builder directory and the observation date.
Does not automatically broaden into semantic, research, A/V, or docs searches for this closed-world request.
avoid: Do NOT turn the directory miss into an absolute claim that Strupey does not exist or has no Stellar history.
Do NOT force an open-world research pass when the user asked only about one named directory.
Do NOT substitute a semantic neighbor or unrelated namesake for an exact builder record.
golden.notes: Closed-world negative control paired with q-edge-strupey-ambiguous-stellar-history. The same token should stop at Scout scope here but trigger one bounded broad pass for the open-world history question.

sources.note: Observed 2026-07-13: returned=0 and total=0 for the exact directory query.
Current exposed operation contract identifies this as the Scout builder-directory surface.

corroboration.claim: Scout's builder directory returned no exact Strupey record on 2026-07-13.

evidence.note: Directory response reported zero returned and zero total.

verified.by: Coordinator from live Scout directory probe
evidence: Paired open-world Strupey truth matrices in Solo scratchpad 611.
rootCause: A broad-recovery policy needs a paired closed-world control so it does not over-search every exact directory miss.
